note: Candidate guardrails for future pumped-fn frames. Candidate-only until ratified into a frame; each was learned from a real post-green catch this run.

anti_goals.metric: project lint (pumped-lite-lint) diagnostics == 0 on all produced code, wired as a package script
anti_goals.context: any pumped-fn code production (src, examples, tests)
anti_goals.learned_from: example passed 11 tests + typecheck while carrying 5 composition errors + 2 port warnings; lint had never been invoked (PKR-8e)

anti_goals.metric: claimed-runnable entrypoints executed end-to-end at least once before acceptance (exit 0, expected output observed)
anti_goals.learned_from: main.ts could not execute at all (entry guard + extensionless imports) despite green suites; found only when audited

anti_goals.metric: test/demo data in production entrypoints == 0; fixtures live in fixtures/ and enter through real input boundaries (stdin/files/queues)
anti_goals.learned_from: demo invoices hardcoded in main.ts; user: nobody adds test data to main

anti_goals.metric: functions with Scope/ExecutionContext/handle parameters outside composition roots == 0 (grep-verifiable); root privilege = create scope, exec flows, dispose
anti_goals.learned_from: runIngest(scope)/reviewQueue(scope), then the glue re-hid inside main.ts under the composition-root exemption; two rounds to eradicate

anti_goals.metric: pumped/no-direct-flow-composition == 0; flows compose via deps handles (exec/execStream), never closure imports
anti_goals.context: flows; enforced by lint-zero-gate but worth naming — a missing API for the idiom (FlowHandle.execStream) is a core-gap finding, not an excuse
anti_goals.learned_from: 5 closure compositions; deps-declared streaming was impossible until execStream was added to FlowHandle

anti_goals.metric: every path where a stream carries must-not-drop work has a burst regression test (N pushes mid-consumption, each item lands exactly once)
anti_goals.context: any consumer of conflating channels (resolveStream/changes); misuse of ratified semantics, not implementation bugs
anti_goals.learned_from: orchestrator-specced ingest over resolveStream silently dropped invoice batches under burst

anti_goals.metric: hand-rolled type gymnastics, never-executed scaffolding, manual iterator driving where for await is natural, verbose test names == 0 at acceptance
anti_goals.context: all tests; use expectTypeOf (it evaluates its argument — assert on real values)
anti_goals.learned_from: typeContracts ceremony + [Symbol.asyncIterator]().next() chains survived A4 because the wall only named source

anti_goals.metric: frame style walls explicitly list: source, tests, type-parameter naming, boundary rules; unenumerated surface == assumed unguarded
anti_goals.learned_from: four post-green style catches (test ceremony, iterator driving, T-prefixes, scope functions) all fell outside A4 as written

anti_goals.metric: every rejection heuristic gets an explicit false-positive pass (legit inputs it might reject) before acceptance
anti_goals.learned_from: F3 rejected scalar outputs with a .next method; needed two narrowings

anti_goals.metric: every LOC ceiling paired with a mandatory compression round (discovery sweep + apply, revert-dont-weaken boundary) before acceptance
anti_goals.learned_from: 387/400 held numerically yet user pushed too-much-code twice; ceiling bounds growth, never demands compression (-99 was available)
